problem text: Determine the number of digits in $99^{99}$.
problem solution: The number $99^{99}$ contains 198 digits. We have $99^{99} = 99^{99} \times \left(\frac{100}{100}\right)^{99} = 100^{99} \times \left(\frac{99}{100}\right)^{99} = 10^{198} \times \left(1-\frac{1}{100}\right)^{99} \approx 10^{198} \times \left(1-\frac{1}{100}\right)^{100} \approx 10^{198} \times \frac{1}{e}$. Consequently, $99^{99}$ has 198 digits.
problem hint: The provided text with the specified formatting is: $ (1-\frac{1}{n})^n $ converges to $ \frac{1}{e} $ as $ n $ becomes very large. How can you adjust $ 99^{99} $ to match this expression?
